explanation: This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION), then eventually within N_DURATION time units, it will transition to the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL), given that the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good).

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state within N_DURATION time units (bool_exp3). The condition for this transition is that the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The formula uses a globally (G) enforced implication to ensure this behavior in all reachable states.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units, given that the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The chosen template ensures the implication holds globally across all time steps. The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks for the TRANSITION state; bool_exp3 checks for the NOMINAL state while considering system support and sensor data quality. The template ID uses a Globally checked implication to enforce that the behavior is consistent across all time steps.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units, provided that the system is supported (supported) and the sensor data is good (good).  The implication (->) ensures the transition happens only under the specified conditions. The G operator ensures this holds globally, meaning this should be true for all time.

The chosen template is a good fit because it ensures the event will occur within a reasonable timeframe and handles the case where it might not happen at all, preventing false positives.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state within N_DURATION time units (bool_exp3). The condition for this transition is that the system is supported and sensor data is good.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state within N_DURATION time units, provided the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The formula uses the implication operator to express the conditional requirement.  It ensures that if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state and the conditions are met (supported and good are true), the next state must be NOMINAL. The G operator ensures this condition holds globally (at all times).

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state within 2 time units, provided that the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The chosen template captures this eventual behavior with a bounded time constraint. The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in the TRANSITION state, and bool_exp3 represents the combined condition that the system is supported and sensor data is good and the state is NOMINAL. The N_DURATION is set to 2.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it eventually transitions to the NOMINAL state within N_DURATION time units, provided the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The formula uses implication (->) to express the conditional behavior. The antecedent (bool_exp2) represents the condition where the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state.  The consequent (bool_exp3) expresses that it will eventually reach NOMINAL. The formula ensures this transition happens within the specified time window. The atomic proposition "supported" and "good" are directly used, reflecting the requirements.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state within 2 time units, provided the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The property uses template 14. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION).  bool_exp3 represents the condition that the system is supported and the sensor data is good (supported & good). The F[0,N_DURATION] operator ensures that the transition to NOMINAL occurs within N_DURATION time units, which is set to 2 to capture the "within 2 time units" requirement. The G operator guarantees this behavior holds globally.

This captures the requirement that the autopilot changes states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL under specified conditions.

G ensures that the implication holds globally, and F[0, N_DURATION] indicates that the transition to NOMINAL must occur within the specified time window.

The condition for this transition is that the system is supported and the sensor data is good. Thus, bool_exp2 is state_is_TRANSITION and bool_exp3 is (supported & good) & state_is_NOMINAL.

The use of G ensures that this property holds globally, i.e., at all times. The use of -> (implies) is to state that under the condition (antecedent) of being in TRANSITION state, the consequent (transition to NOMINAL) must happen. F[0, N_DURATION] specifies that the event must happen eventually in at most N_DURATION time units.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that when the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state and the system is supported and sensor data is good, it eventually transitions to the NOMINAL state within N_DURATION time units.  bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION), the system is supported (supported), and the sensor data is good (good). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL). The G operator ensures that this implication always holds. The F operator indicates that the NOMINAL state will eventually be reached. The [0,N_DURATION] interval constraint specifies that the transition must occur within N_DURATION time units. If the transition doesn't happen within N_DURATION time units, the formula may still hold if the autopilot remains in TRANSITION state or the precondition is not satisfied.

The formula uses the implication operator to express the conditional behavior, ensuring the transition to NOMINAL occurs only when the specified conditions are met.
